title: 7 Oversold Stocks Due for a Massive Short Squeeze
text: Short squeeze stocks can provide particular opportunities no matter what the market is doing. The market has declined in August as traders have taken some profits after a strong first half to the year.

title: Maui Wildfires: Investors Assess Hawaii-Related Stocks, Power Provider Faces Lawsuit
text: Devastating Maui wildfires have placed Hawaii-related stocks on investors' radar. Hawaii Electric faces lawsuit for role in blaze.

title: Host Hotels & Resorts: Stable Company With A Positive Future Outlook
text: Host Hotels & Resorts is a hotel REIT focused on upper upscale rooms. The company has shown a strong recovery since the pandemic and the guidance suggests HST will perform well going forward. I present my bullish analysis of the stock.

title: The 3 Most Undervalued ESG Stocks to Buy Now: August 2023
text: Diving into the world of Environmental, Social, and Governance, seeking undervalued ESG stocks can feel like hunting for unicorns. Some investors believe that many of the ESG stocks to buy can soothe their consciences but don't outperform the market.

title: Losers Of REIT Earnings Season
text: After covering the Winners of REIT Earnings Season last week, Part 2 of our Earnings Recap focuses on the worst-performing property sectors and common threads shared by these laggards. While there were upside standouts and some solid reports within these lagging property sectors, the losers of REIT earnings season included: Self-Storage, Hotel, Healthcare, and Specialty REITs. Many of the "misses" emanated from the direct and secondary effects of the higher interest rate environment, underscoring the continued challenges facing more-highly-levered private real estate portfolios.

title: 3 REITs Raising Their Dividend Game: Is it Time to Buy?
text: Real estate investment trusts (REITs) have had a challenging go of it in the last few years. That meant smaller dividend increases.

title: Host Hotels & Resorts: Strong Balance Sheet, Stable Dividends
text: Host Hotels & Resorts recently released its Q2 2023 results, increasing its dividends by 25%. The company has a strong balance sheet. There is the potential for future dividend increases, given the current low dividend payout ratio.

title: Wildfires Could Hit Hawaii Tourism Industry. Some Stocks to Watch.
text: The biggest question for the island's companies—and broader economy— will be how long tourists might stay away.

title: Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (HST) Q2 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (NASDAQ:HST ) Q2 2023 Earnings Conference Call August 3, 2023 1:00 PM ET Company Participants Jaime Marcus - SVP, IR Jim Risoleo - President and CEO Sourav Ghosh - EVP and CFO Conference Call Participants Duane Pfennigwerth - Evercore ISI Aryeh Klein - BMO Capital Markets Anthony Powell - Barclays Smedes Rose - Citi David Katz - Jefferies Michael Bellisario - Baird Bill Crow - Raymond James Tyler Batory - Oppenheimer Chris Woronka - Deutsche Bank Robin Farley - UBS Dori Kesten - Wells Fargo Operator Good afternoon, and welcome to the Host Hotels & Resorts Second Quarter 2023 Earnings Conference Call. Today's conference is being recorded.

title: Host Hotels & Resorts Provides Updated Second Quarter 2023 Investor Presentation
text: BETHESDA, Md., Aug. 03, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (NASDAQ: HST) (the “Company”), the nation's largest lodging real estate investment trust, today provided an updated investor presentation for second quarter 2023 results. The investor presentation can be found on the Investor Relations section on the Company's website at https://www.hosthotels.com/#key-investors-materials.

title: Host Hotels' (HST) Q2 FFO & Revenues Lag, '23 View Narrowed
text: Host Hotels (HST) posts lackluster Q2 results on lower-than-anticipated revenues. Moderating rates at the company's resorts hamper comparable hotel RevPAR growth.

title: Host Hotels (HST) Reports Q2 Earnings: What Key Metrics Have to Say
text: The headline numbers for Host Hotels (HST) give insight into how the company performed in the quarter ended June 2023, but it may be worthwhile to compare some of its key metrics to Wall Street estimates and the year-ago actuals.

title: Host Hotels (HST) Q2 FFO and Revenues Lag Estimates
text: Host Hotels (HST) came out with quarterly funds from operations (FFO) of $0.53 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.56 per share. This compares to FFO of $0.58 per share a year ago.

text: The Ritz-Carlton, Naples Reopens Completion of the Marriott Transformational Capital Program
